data.title: Long-running sessions dispatch generic sub-agents for code review instead of named dso:code-reviewer-* agents
data.description: In long-running sessions (especially after context compaction), the orchestrator dispatches generic-purpose sub-agents for code review instead of the named dso:code-reviewer-* agents specified in CLAUDE.md. This causes reviews to use incorrect prompts, skip the tier selection logic, and produce findings that don't conform to the reviewer-findings.json contract. The named agent system was designed specifically for this — each agent has a model assignment and specialized prompt — but the dispatch is being bypassed. This is a chronic recurring issue that has persisted across multiple sessions and host projects. Root cause is likely Instruction Locality: the rule to use named agents is documented in CLAUDE.md but the execution site (review dispatch) lacks a co-located reminder. The fix should add an enforcement reminder at the point in the review workflow where sub-agent dispatch occurs.
